g.text: [the file is inside a git repository]
sidebar shows one tree per project
across ALL projects, keep files whose
Every local open sends a tree — a git root when there is one,
else the containing directory as a
`send-tree!` is reached, so they synthesize nothing.
Containment therefore only ever removes synthetic roots: a repo
nested inside a browsed directory stays its own project.
A synthetic root covered by another does not duplicate it — its
walked subtree is re-based and merged in, so the file you just
opened is always in the tree (the `--others` invariant).
Removal is manual — project header ⟶ *Remove from Files*.
